checks.a_analysis: I have compared every paragraph and list item in the JSON output against the provided source PDF images and OCR text. Every piece of text in the output, from the introductory paragraphs to the numbered preliminary questions, is present in the source document. There are no invented sections or hallucinated content.

The document contains one email address: PSAPeligibilityrequest@nc.gov. I verified this against the source document on page 1. The output correctly captures the email address and provides an appropriate mailto link. No other contact information is present in the document.

The source document consists of two pages. The JSON output includes all content from page 1 (procedure description and item list) and page 2 (maintenance note continuation and the six preliminary questions). The split paragraph across pages 1 and 2 ('...as some percentages may apply to the maintenance...') was correctly reconstructed as a single continuous paragraph in the output.
The source document contains no tables; it consists of body text, a bulleted list, and an ordered list. Therefore, this check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: All sections of the document are complete. I cross-referenced the content of the unordered list on page 1 and the ordered list of questions on page 2. All items and questions are present in their entirety without truncation.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text content matches the source document faithfully. Formatting choices, such as representing the diamond bullets as an unordered list and accurately capturing bold/underline/italic styles, are within acceptable parameters. No wording changes that alter meaning were detected.

checks.a_analysis: The output document correctly represents the content of the two-page source document without duplication. Each paragraph, list item, and heading appears exactly once, matching the source's structure. Paragraph 4, which spans from the bottom of page 1 to the top of page 2, is correctly merged into a single continuous paragraph in the JSON output.

The heading hierarchy is logical and follows the structure of the source document. The document title is used as the section title (effectively level 1), and the subsequent heading 'Preliminary Questions to Consider Before Submitting a Request:' is correctly assigned level 2. No heading levels are skipped.
There are no tables in the source document.

The reading order in the JSON output matches the visual flow of the source PDF. It begins with the title, follows with the introductory paragraphs, lists the required documentation (unordered list), includes the advisory italicized paragraph, and then moves to the maintenance paragraph and the final checklist (ordered list) found on page 2.

The document contains two distinct lists. The first list, identified by diamond bullets in the source, is correctly converted into an unordered list with 4 items. The second list, numbered 1-6 in the source, is correctly converted into an ordered list with 6 items. The nesting and item boundaries are preserved accurately.

The email address 'PSAPeligibilityrequest@nc.gov' is correctly identified as a mailto link in the JSON output. No other links were found in the source, and none were incorrectly injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully replicates the visual organization of the source PDF. The title, introductory text, bulleted list of requirements, maintenance-specific paragraph, and the final numbered list of preliminary questions all appear in the same logical order as the original document. While the PDF is split into two pages, the HTML correctly presents this as a single continuous document, which is expected for web rendering.
The source PDF does not contain any tables; therefore, no table rendering issues are present in the HTML output.
The source document contains no meaningful images, charts, or logos. The content is entirely text-based. The HTML output correctly represents all text elements from the source.
There is no evidence of visual content duplication in the HTML output. Each paragraph, list item, and heading appears exactly once, corresponding to its single appearance in the source PDF.
